zh.lifeSummary: 當Christiana Clarke Parnell  (ggmother of Lizzie Borden)於大約1515年出生在英國時，她的父親Robert Williamson Clarke21歲，母親Catherine Clarke7歲。她於1535年與Parnell在英國倫敦結婚。她去世於大約1570年在英國倫敦享年57歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可英蓋斯同。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Christiana Clarke Parnell  (ggmother of Lizzie Borden)于大约1515年出生在英国时，她的父亲Robert Williamson Clarke21岁，母亲Catherine Clarke7岁。她于1535年与Parnell在英国倫敦结婚。她去世于大约1570年在英国倫敦享年57岁，埋葬在英国英格兰艾可英蓋斯同。

nameMeanings.name: Parnell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Pernel, Parnell (Old French Peronel(le)) from Latin Petronilla, a feminine pet form of the Latin personal name Petronius. Petronilla was the name of a supposed early Christian saint and martyr. This surname was also brought to Ireland from Cheshire in the 17th century, by a family who had been supporters of Cromwell's Parliamentarians during the English Civil War. Charles Stewart Parnell, who founded the Irish Parliamentary Party in 1882, was a descendant of this family. See also Pennell .

variants.name: Penn
meanings: English: habitational name from either of two places called Penn (in Buckinghamshire and Staffordshire), both of which are named with Brittonic penn ‘head’, often used in hill-names.

English: topographic name or metonymic occupational name from Middle English pen(n) ‘enclosure, animal pen, fold’ (Old English penn), for someone who lived by or worked at a fold. Examples of this surname are formally difficult to distinguish from those in 1 above.
English: from a pet form of the female personal name Pernel, see Parnell .

variants.name: Pernell
meanings: English (Middlesex and Warwickshire): variant of   Parnell  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Purnell
meanings: English: variant of   Parnell  .

variants.name: Pennell
meanings: English (especially southeastern):
metronym from the Middle English female personal name Pennel (an assimilated form of Parnell ).

habitational name from Penn Hall in Pensax (Worcestershire), or from either of two places called Penhill in Devon and Yorkshire, or possibly from Pendle in Lancashire. All of the placenames probably derive from British penn ‘head’ + Old English hyll ‘hill’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Lizzie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore.
Italian: from the personal name Lizzio, a variant of Lizio, recorded in the medieval Latinized form Litius.

variants.name: Lizzi
meanings: Italian:
alternatively, a topographic name from the Salentino dialect word lizzo, plural lizzi ‘holm oak, ilex’.
patronymic or plural form of   Lizzio  .

nameMeanings.name: Borden)
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Sussex): habitational name from Borden in Kent or Bordon in Hampshire. The one in Kent is perhaps derived from Old English bord ‘board, plank’ or bor ‘hill’ + denu ‘valley’ or denn ‘(swine) pasture’. Bordon in Hampshire is ‘Burdon's valley’, from the Middle English surname Burdon + Middle English dene ‘valley’ (Old English denu).

variants.name: Bordon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Alicia, Armando, Carlos, Cristina, Enrique, Ernesto, Francisco, Jose, Julio, Luis, Marcelo.  Italian Alessandra, Dario, Elio, Umberto.
Spanish (Bordón): from bordón ‘pilgrim's staff’, hence a nickname for a pilgrim.
Spanish (Bordón): habitational name from a place so named in Teruel province.

nameMeanings.name: Christiana
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval learned feminine form of Christian . As a recent revival it represents an elaborated form of Christina . It is also sometimes spelled Christianna , under the influence of the name Anna .

nameMeanings.name: Clarke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which originated as an occupational name denoting a clerk (Latin clericus), in the Middle Ages a man in minor holy orders who earned his living by his ability to read and write. It is recorded as a given name in Britain from the late 17th century onwards. Since the 1930s, when it was associated particularly with the film star Clark Gable ( 1901–60 ), it has been widely used as a given name, especially in North America.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Christiana Clarke Parnell  (ggmother of Lizzie Borden) was born about 1515, in England, her father, Robert Williamson Clarke, was 21 and her mother, Catherine Clarke, was 7. She married Parnell in 1535, in London, England. She died about 1570, in London, England, at the age of 57, and was buried in Ingatestone, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Parnell en 1535, à Londres, Angleterre. Elle est décédée environ 1570, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterrée à Ingatestone, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Ela casou-se com Parnell em 1535, em London, Inglaterra. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1570, em London, Inglaterra, com 57 anos, e foi sepultada em Ingatestone, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.St Mary Aldermanbury, London, England: ст мэри алдирманбури, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Christiana Clarke Parnell  (ggmother of Lizzie Borden) родилась около 1515 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Robert Williamson Clarke было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Catherine Clarke, 7. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Parnell в 1535 г. в местности Лондон, Англия. Она умерла около 1570 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 57 лет и была похоронена в местности ингэитестоун, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.
